title: Short-horizon sequential stint dynamics
summary: Public F1 models repeatedly use rolling mean/std/gradient features over recent laps, implying that immediate trajectory change matters more than absolute tyre age alone; the current top local recipes mostly use static ratios and hand-set windows.
rationale: Public F1 models repeatedly use rolling mean/std/gradient features over recent laps, implying that immediate trajectory change matters more than absolute tyre age alone; the current top local recipes mostly use static ratios and hand-set windows.
implementation_hint: Within each Year/Race/Driver/Stint, create lag-1/2/3 features and rolling slope/volatility for `LapTime_Delta`, lap-time z-score, incremental degradation, and position change; reset all windows at pit stops and generate features with strict past-only transforms.
expected_effect: Captures undercut/overcut pressure and sudden performance drop-offs before the stop, complementing the existing service-window heuristics.
risk: If the underlying rows are not perfectly ordered by lap within driver-race sequences, these features can silently leak or become noisy.
